summary: Verified all 5 targeted changes (renames, hero font, colored logos, /dashboard/connections page, sidebar reorder). 16/16 final checks PASS (initial 38/45 had 7 failures all due to test-script issues — wrong selectors / reading input.value vs innerText / top-bar h1 vs page main h1 — not actual bugs). Login regression (test@jalwa.com/test1234) still works → /dashboard. Logout now correctly redirects to '/' (the iteration_2 logout bug is fixed via isLoggingOutRef pattern in DashboardLayout).

issues: Recharts emits multiple 'width(-1) height(-1)' console warnings on initial mount of the LineChart due to ResponsiveContainer measuring before layout. Cosmetic only; chart renders fine after mount. Could be silenced by giving the parent an explicit min-height or aspect ratio.

Top-bar h1 is hard-coded to 'Dashboard' on every page (data-testid=dash-page-title). It does not update to reflect the current route (AI Visibility, Growth Score, Connections, etc.). Page main h1 inside <Outlet/> is correct, but top-bar title is stale. Minor UX inconsistency — recommend deriving it from the active route or a per-page title prop.

change_1_renames.dashboard_home: Shows 'Your Growth Score' (not 'Your Jalwa Score'); 'View full score breakdown' link navigates to /dashboard/growth-score
change_1_renames.public_pages_clean: HomePage modules, Features 4 modules, Pricing feature list, AboutPage, Footer 4 product links — no Jalwa Pulse/Write/Publish/Post/Score anywhere

change_4_connections_page.ghost_save_connect_flow: Clicking Save & Connect toasts and converts card to Connected state; Disconnect reverts back
change_4_connections_page.social_x_twitter_modal: Has 'redirected to X / Twitter' text, 'Connect with X / Twitter' button with black bg rgb(0,0,0), Cancel button

action_items: (Low priority) Fix Recharts ResponsiveContainer warnings on /dashboard/growth-score by giving the parent an explicit min-height or aspect ratio.
(Low priority) Make top-bar h1 (data-testid=dash-page-title) dynamic per route instead of hard-coded 'Dashboard'.
critical_code_review_comments: DashboardLayout still has dead Trophy/Plug imports usage is fine, but verify all 8 nav icons render (LayoutDashboard, Trophy, Radar, Pen, Send, Share2, Plug, Settings) — confirmed in DOM.

PlatformLogo.jsx defines testids like platform-logo-x-twitter for 'X / Twitter' (the regex collapses ' / ' to a single '-'). Earlier review request hinted at testid 'platform-logo-x---twitter' which is incorrect — the actual generated value is 'platform-logo-x-twitter'. Update any docs/specs referencing the triple-dash variant.

ConnectionsPage uses POST_DATA.socialAccounts as initial state for socialAccounts — coupling between pages. If POST_DATA changes order/labels, this page's SocialModal testid generation will follow. OK for now but worth a comment.
Recharts ResponsiveContainer width/height warnings spammed in console — set min-height on parent to silence.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (16/16 final checks pass; initial 7 failures were test-selector issues, not bugs)

test_credentials: test@jalwa.com / test1234 (dummy mode — any credentials accepted)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 targeted changes verified working. Key testids confirmed: dash-nav-{dashboard,growth-score,ai-visibility,ai-writer,auto-publish,social-autopilot,connections,settings}; growth-score-page; connections-page; connections-website-section; connections-social-section; website-card-{wordpress,shopify,webflow,ghost,hubspot,wix,squarespace,notion,next-js}; social-card-{instagram,facebook,linkedin,x-twitter,pinterest,youtube} (NOT x---twitter); modal-{wordpress,shopify,ghost,hubspot,wix,squarespace,notion,next-js}; modal-social-{instagram,facebook,linkedin,x-twitter,pinterest,youtube}; platform-logo-{name}. When reading inputs in modals, use input.value not innerText. When selecting page H1, scope to 'main h1' since top-bar always shows 'Dashboard'.
rca of the issue: None — no real bugs found. The 7 false-fail tests in the first run were due to my own selectors: (1) sidebar query ran before NavLink render — fix: wait_for_selector dash-nav-dashboard; (2) page H1 via document.querySelector('h1') picked top-bar h1 instead of page h1 — fix: scope to 'main h1'; (3) WordPress modal key was inside <Input value=...> not in innerText — fix: read input.value; (4) X/Twitter card/modal testid is x-twitter (single dash) not x---twitter.
